_doc: The family registry — per-FAMILY default capability set + parser/profile/template choices. CAPABILITY-RESOLUTION-DESIGN-CORRECTED.md §3. registry-is-truth (AGENTS.md rule 8): this FILE is the single source; runtime/capabilities/resolver.py loads it at import (fail-loud). This KILLS the hand-repeated flags: chat-4b/chat-2b/chat-08b repeat the IDENTICAL flag list, and embed-bge/embed-jina-v5/embed-qwen3 repeat ANOTHER identical list — adding a fourth member meant copy-pasting it and risking a silently-dropped flag. Now a model declares `family: <name>` and inherits the whole set. Adding a FAMILY = adding ONE entry HERE -> all its models inherit (no code edit). A family row: `stack` (the default expression stack) · `provides` (the capability TAGS its models declare) · `capabilities` (the ORDERED list of serve_ref keys whose stack-flag-fragments serve_flags() emits — the order IS the canonical launch order, matching today's hand-authored sequence) · `serve_params` (the {placeholder} values the stack templates fill: tool_parser/chat_template/reasoning_parser/...) · `fields` (Layer-B field defaults). A per-model `capability_overrides` ⊕ on top of this (override wins) -> the model's effective set. SHIPPING shape (LIVE, build-log 01-serving): the qwen3.5 family's `reasoning` capability emits `--reasoning-parser qwen3` (the real reasoning fix) and the model uses its OWN stock chat template (NO --chat-template flag); the hand-rolled chat_template_nothink.jinja is RETIRED, the per-request enable_thinking toggle lives in fabric/transport.py (C3). This is the full implementation, not the retired reproduce-today.

qwen3.5._note: the local Qwen3.5 chat workers (services.json chat-4b/chat-2b/chat-08b AWQ + chat-4b-fp8). SHIPPING shape (LIVE, NOT the retired reproduce-today): regenerates --enable-prefix-caching --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_xml --reasoning-parser qwen3 --trust-remote-code, in canonical order (capability_types.json `order` ranks). The hand-rolled chat_template_nothink.jinja is RETIRED — each model uses its OWN stock thinking-capable template (NO --chat-template flag) + per-request enable_thinking (fabric/transport.py); reasoning is SERVED by --reasoning-parser qwen3 which splits </think> into message.reasoning (QWEN35-REASONING-SPIKE-FINDINGS.md). chat-4b-fp8 adds `vision: lm-only` as a per-model capability_override (→ --language-model-only, between reasoning and trust_remote_code) for VRAM; the AWQ workers do NOT (no --language-model-only). vision is NOT a family default. The capabilities list IS the canonical launch order; an enum/bool capability_override is injected at its `order` rank.

embed-pooling._note: the vLLM pooling-runner embedders (services.json embed-bge/embed-jina-v5/embed-qwen3) — IDENTICAL hand-authored flags today: --runner pooling --trust-remote-code. This family regenerates exactly that, in that order. (custom-server embedders embed-pplx/embed-jina-v4 are NOT this family — they run bespoke scripts, see stack_capabilities.json custom-server.)

nemotron._note: the NVIDIA Nemotron MoE chat worker (services.json chat-nemotron — stelterlab/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ). DIFFERENT model family from qwen3.5 — its own serve contract: --quantization compressed-tensors --cpu-offload-gb 6 --enforce-eager --trust-remote-code (no tool-call-parser / no reasoning-parser today). Reproduces its current hand-authored flags BYTE-FOR-BYTE. The compressed-tensors quantization + CPU-offload + enforce-eager are this model's bespoke needs (the `ninja`/enforce-eager history lived here); declared as a minimal family so the model lights up its real serve flags without being forced onto qwen3.5.
